raw.objective: “This study represented a serious attempt to quantify the effects of illness, demographics, and polypharmacy on intravenous phenytoin kinetics. Models with linear and Michaelis-Menten elimination, as well as models with differing modes of induction, were compared”
raw.covariates: Tested: HT, WT, SEX, AGE, CLcr, malignancy, phenytoin history, comedication with fluconazole, isoniazid, cimetidine, cotrimoxazole, CBZ, fluoxetine, PHB, tricyclic antidepressants overdose, nafcillin, ceftriaxone, VPA. Included: WT, phenytoin history (induction) on Vm
raw.observations: Although the authors describe their work as failing to describe the PHT PK, their goodness-of-fit plot shows adequate predictions. Theirs is the only model describing the possible induction of PHT elimination. The impact of a temperature higher than 100 fahrenheid was included into the induction description, which means that the effects of induction and raised temperature were not separated from each other
raw.dose: Non-steady-state oral and i.v. doses in patients
raw.structure: 1 compartment model with zero-order absorption or infusion and michaelis menten elimination
raw.equations: IF = theta7*exp(eta1) MT=theta8*exp(eta2) k=theta9 Z=0 if(time>MT) Z=1 Vm = theta1*(WT/70)*(1+Z*IF*(theta2**TM)*(1-exp(-k*(time-MT)))) *exp(eta3) Vd = theta3*WT/(1+theta4*ALB)*exp(eta4) Km = theta5*Vd*(1+theta6**TCA) dA/dt = R-(Vm*A(1))/(A(1)+Km)) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1)+eps2 TM: is temperature of patient above 100 fahrenheid (37.8 celcius)? (0=no; 1=yes) ALB is albumin in g/dl
raw.parameter_values: theta1=24.5 theta2=2.39 theta3=3.3 theta4=0.82 theta5=7.92 theta6=3.55 theta7=0.39 theta8=59.5 theta9=0.0061 omega1=0.11 omega2=0.16 omega3=3.07 omega4=0.014 sigma1=0.0053 sigma2=1.12

covariates.raw: Tested: HT, WT, SEX, AGE, CLcr, malignancy, phenytoin history, comedication with fluconazole, isoniazid, cimetidine, cotrimoxazole, CBZ, fluoxetine, PHB, tricyclic antidepressants overdose, nafcillin, ceftriaxone, VPA. Included: WT, phenytoin history (induction) on Vm

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
